prompt: You ran ADMIXTURE at K=5 on 3,010 rice accessions and the QQ plot of your subsequent GWAS for flowering time shows genomic inflation (lambda_GC = 1.28). You used PC1-PC2 as covariates. Diagnose the problem and propose a fix.
context:
GWAS setup:
  Model: GEMMA linear mixed model
  Covariates: PC1, PC2 (from full-panel PCA)
  GRM: Built from 200K LD-pruned SNPs (all chromosomes)
  Phenotype: days to flowering (quantitative)
  Panel: 3,010 accessions, 5 subpopulations
  Lambda_GC: 1.28 (expected: ~1.00-1.05)

Phenotype distribution by subpopulation:
  indica: mean 95 days (SD 12)
  trop_japonica: mean 108 days (SD 14)
  temp_japonica: mean 82 days (SD 8)
  aus: mean 88 days (SD 10)
  aromatic: mean 104 days (SD 16)

Note: Flowering time is strongly correlated with subpopulation identity (F-statistic = 48.2, p < 1e-50)
correct_answer:
Diagnosis: The genomic inflation (lambda=1.28) is caused by RESIDUAL POPULATION STRATIFICATION not captured by PC1-PC2 alone. While the GRM in the mixed model captures some relatedness, using only 2 PCs as fixed covariates leaves substantial structure uncorrected. With 5 deeply divergent subpopulations and flowering time strongly correlated with subpopulation (F=48.2), even small residual stratification creates genome-wide inflation.

Specific issues:
(1) TOO FEW PCs: PC1-PC2 capture 46.5% of structure variance but miss PC3-PC6 which capture aus-aromatic differentiation and within-subpopulation structure. Since flowering time varies across ALL subpopulations (not just indica vs japonica), PC3-PC6 contain confounding signal.

(2) GRM INCLUDES TEST CHROMOSOME: If the GRM was built from all chromosomes including the test chromosome, proximal contamination can inflate test statistics near true QTL while under-correcting elsewhere.

Fixes (implement ALL):
(1) INCREASE PCs: Include PC1-PC6 (or PC1-PC10) as fixed covariates. The Tracy-Widom test identified 6 significant PCs for this dataset.

(2) LOCO-GRM: Rebuild the GRM excluding the chromosome being tested (leave-one-chromosome-out). This prevents proximal contamination and improves both power at true QTL and deflation elsewhere.

(3) VERIFY WITH QQ PLOT: After implementing fixes, lambda_GC should drop to 1.00-1.05. If still inflated, consider: (a) adding subpopulation indicator variables as fixed effects, (b) running within-subpopulation GWAS and meta-analyzing, or (c) using FarmCPU which iteratively adds significant SNPs as covariates.

(4) BIOLOGICAL CONSIDERATION: Flowering time has known major-effect QTL (Ghd7, Hd1, DTH8) that may create genuine widespread LD-based inflation. After structure correction, remaining inflation at true QTL is expected and should not be further corrected -- use the median-based lambda (lambda_GC at median rather than mean) to assess residual stratification without being affected by true signals.
rubric: Full marks (3): Must diagnose insufficient PCs as primary cause, recommend increasing to PC1-PC6+, suggest LOCO-GRM, and note the distinction between structure inflation and true signal inflation. Mostly correct (2): Identifies too few PCs and recommends increasing but missing LOCO or the true-signal caveat. Partial (1): Identifies inflation without specific diagnosis. Incorrect (0): Recommends removing the GRM or using no structure correction.
notes: Tests GWAS troubleshooting for genomic inflation -- the most common QC issue in multi-subpopulation GWAS.
